title: List Cycle
description:
Given a linked list, return the node where the cycle begins. If there is no cycle, return null.

Try solving it using constant additional space.

Example:
Input:
     ______
     |   |
     \/  |
1 -> 2 -> 3 -> 4

Return the node corresponding to node 3.

Note: This is function only problem. You are required to just complete given function.
Input Reading and Output printing is taken care by hidden instructor code.
inputFormat:
The first line of input contains an integer T, number of testcases.
For each test case:
- The first line contains two integers: N (size of the linked list) and k (index of the node where the cycle starts, or -1 if no cycle exists).
- The second line contains N integers, the elements of the linked list.

Input reading is handled by hidden code.
outputFormat:
Return the node from where cycle starts in the linked list. If there is no cycle, return null.

Output printing is handled by hidden code.

title: Remove Cycle in Linked List
description: Given a linked list of integers where last node might be pointing to any of the previous nodes in the list or NULL. Write a function that removes the cycle, if found, and makes last node point to NULL.
inputFormat:
First test case contains integer T - number of test cases. Each test case consists of the following input format.

Test Case Line 1: N K - Number of nodes in linked list and 0-index based position to which last node of linked list is pointing. K = -1 in case there is no cycle in the list.
Test Case Line 2: N space separated integers, representing values in the nodes of linked list.
outputFormat: For each test case, print the linked list node values (going left to right), space separated. Each test case output should be on a separate line.

title: Cycle Length in Linked List
description: Given a linked list of integers where last node might be pointing to any of the previous nodes in the list or NULL. Write a function that finds the length of the cycle i.e. number of nodes that are part of the cycle, if found, return 0 otherwise.
inputFormat:
First line of input contains a single integer T - number of test cases.

First line of each test case contains two space separated integers N and K, where N is number of nodes in the linked list and K is the 0 based position of the node to which the last node of linked list will point to.

Next line contains N space separated integers denoting the linked list elements.
outputFormat: For each test case print the length of the cycle if there is a cycle in the linked list else print 0. Output for each test case should be on a new line.

title: Palindrome List
description:
Given a singly linked list, determine if it is a palindrome.

Expected solution is linear in time and constant in space.

List 1->2->1 is a palindrome.
List 1->2->3 is not a palindrome.
inputFormat: Input reading is already taken care by hidden code. Just complete the given function.
outputFormat: Output printing is already taken care by hidden code. Just complete the given function.

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description:
Merge two sorted linked lists and return it as a new list.

The new list should be made by splicing together the nodes of the first two lists, and should also be sorted.

For example, given following linked lists:
5 -> 8 -> 20
4 -> 11 -> 15

The merged list should be:
4 -> 5 -> 8 -> 11 -> 15 -> 20

Note: This is function only problem. You are required to just complete given function.
Input reading and output printing is taken care by hidden instructor code.
inputFormat: Input reading is already taken care by hidden code. Just complete the given function.
outputFormat: Output printing is already taken care by hidden code. Just complete the given function.

title: Last Digit of X power N
description: Given a very large number X and integer N, print the last digit of X raised to the power N.
inputFormat:
First line of input contains number of testcases T.
Each testcase contains X and N.
outputFormat: For each test case, print the last digit of X power N.

title: Count Vowels
description: Given a string S, count the number of vowels in the given string. For this problem, we will consider A, E, I, O, U, Y as vowels. A string is given as a non-separated single word as input.
inputFormat: First line contains an integer T. Next T lines contain one word S per line. Input string S can have both uppercase and lowercase characters.
outputFormat: For each test case, print a separate line containing the number of vowels in the given string.

title: Hated Words
description:
Manju wants to improve English, but he hates words that have 5 or more consonants in a row. Now, Manju wants to prepare short notes where he writes all words which are not hated by him.

Note: If the word does not contain 5 or more consonants in a row, then those words are not hated by Manju.

Now his friends suggest N words each of length M.

Help Manju to check if the word given can be written in his short notes or not. Print No if the word is considered as a hated word, otherwise print Yes.
inputFormat: First line of input contains integer N denoting the number of words that Manju’s friends suggest. For each suggestion, there are two lines of input, where the first line denotes length of the word M and the second line is the word W of length M.
outputFormat: For each suggestion, print Yes if the suggested word can be written in the short notes, otherwise print No.
constraints:
1 <= N <= 100
1 <= M <= 100
W contains only lowercase alphabets.

title: Find the thread
description:
Shiva and Isha are coming back from Himachal Pradesh after their Archery training at an Ashram. While coming back on the train, they were super bored and they planned to play a game. But they had very limited resources with which they couldn’t play any game. Shiva came with an idea where he will give Isha a word S and she must find the word thread length of S.

Shiva explained to Isha that word thread length of word S is the maximum length of a non-empty continuous substring in the given word formed by a single character.

Isha wants to win the game at any cost. Help Isha to find the word thread length of S.
inputFormat:
First line contains the number of test cases T.
Next T lines contain string S in a separate line.
String S contains uppercase letters only.
outputFormat: For each test case, output an integer which denotes the word thread length of S.
constraints:
1 <= T <= 10^2
1 <= |S| <= 500
|S| denotes the length of S

title: The Mystery Box
description:
Manju is fond of solving puzzles. He is now trying to open a mystery box. He has to enter N digits such that the code should be an even number. But he is unaware of the numbers so now he asks Geeni to help him. Geeni helps Manju by giving him a number that has N digits, and asks Manju whether that number is helpful for him or not.

A number would be helpful for Manju if he could make an even number by rearranging the digits in that number. Help Manju to check whether the given number is helpful or not.
inputFormat:
The first line of input contains an integer T denoting the number of test cases.
For each test case, there are two lines of input:
- The first line contains an integer N which denotes the required number of digits.
- The second line contains a number that has N digits.
outputFormat: For each test case, print Yes if it is possible to rearrange the digits of the number so that it becomes an even number. Otherwise, print No.

title: Digit Sum Sort
description: Given an array A of size N. You need to sort the contents of this array in ASCENDING ORDER of digit sum of elements. If two numbers have same digit sum, then bigger number should be placed first, smaller should be second.
inputFormat:
First line contains a single integer T - Number of test cases.
Each test case consists of below pattern.
The first line of test case contains a single integer N denoting the size of the array.
The next line contains N space separated integers denoting the contents of the array.
outputFormat:
Print N space separated integers, i.e. the final sorted array.
Each test case prints array on a separate line.

title: Number of Smaller
description:
You are given two arrays A and B of size an and bn respectively. Both arrays are sorted in non-decreasing order. For each element of the second array, find the number of elements in the first array strictly less than it.

NOTE: Can you code the solution in linear time complexity O(an+bn).
inputFormat:
First line contains T - number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains integers an and bn, the sizes of the arrays.
The second line contains an integers ai, elements of the first array, the third line contains bn integers bi, elements of the second array.
outputFormat: For each test case, print a separate line of output containing bn numbers, the number of elements of the first array less than each of bi element of the second array.

title: Merging Arrays
description:
You are given two arrays A and B of size an and bn respectively. Both arrays are sorted in non-decreasing order. Merge them into one sorted array.

PS: Code the solution in linear time O(an+bn).
inputFormat:
First line contains T - number of test cases.

The first line of each test case contains integers an and bn, the sizes of the arrays.

The second line contains an integers ai, elements of the first array, the third line contains bn integers bi, elements of the second array.
outputFormat: For each test case, print a separate line of output containing an+bn integers, the merged array.

title: Min Cost in 2D Matrix
description:
You are given a 2D matrix with N rows and M columns, where each cell value represents the cost to pass through it. Initially you are at the cell (0,0) and you need to go to the bottom most cell, whose co-ordinates are (N-1,M-1).

You can move only RIGHT or DOWN from any given cell.

Out of all the possibilities to complete the journey, you need to choose the path whose sum will be minimum.

At last, print the sum of that path.

(Cost of first and last cell is also included in the total cost that you will calculate).
inputFormat:
First line of input contains an integer T - representing the number of test cases.
For each test case:
- First line contains 2 integers R and C - representing the number of rows and columns in the grid.
- Then next R lines contain C integers each, representing the elements of the grid.
outputFormat: For each test case, print a separate line of output containing minimum cost of the path.

title: Equilibrium Index in Array
description:
Given array A of integers, an index idx is called Equilibrium index if it is a valid index of an element of the array and sum of all the elements to its left is equal to sum of all the elements to its right.

Given an array we need to find the equilibrium index. If no such index exists we say answer is -1. If more than one such index exists, we need to find the rightmost such index.
inputFormat:
Line 1: Integer T - Number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines:
- Test Case Line 1: Integer N - Number of elements in array. If N = 0, next line of array elements will not be given.
- Test Case Line 2: N space separated integers representing array elements.
outputFormat: For each test case print a separate line of output - the equilibrium index for the given array.

title: Sort Age or Shortage
description: There are few folks gathered in India from around the globe. They were asked to punch in their ages into the system. There is shortage of coordinators at the venue so organizers have sent that sheet our way for sorting age-wise. Can you please quickly code a program to sort given ages?
inputFormat:
First line of input contains a single integer N, number of people.
Second line contains N space separated integers denoting the ages of people.
outputFormat: Print given ages in sorted order.

title: Count Unique Triplets with Given Sum
description: Given an array A of size N and a target sum S. Count unique triplets in array that have sum S. Unique means similar triplets should not be counted again.
inputFormat:
Line 1: Integer T - Number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains two integers N and S.
The second line of each test case contains N elements of array A.
outputFormat: For each test case print a separate line of output containing a single integer, the number of unique triplets having sum S.

title: Rotate Elements of Array
description:
Given an array of N integers, rotate elements of the array K times, which is equivalent to right shifting the array elements K times, considering the array as a circular array.

For example:
- For N = 5, K = 1, one right shift of [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] gives [5, 1, 2, 3, 4].
- For N = 4, K = 2, two right shifts of [1, 3, 5, 7] gives [5, 7, 1, 3].

Note: Make changes in the given array itself.
inputFormat:
Input reading and output printing is taken care, just implement the given function.

Line 1: Integer T - Number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines:
- TC Line 1: Integers N and K - size of array and number of rotations.
- TC Line 2: N space separated integers.
outputFormat:
Input reading and output printing is taken care, just implement the given function.

For each test case, print the array after K rotations (K right shifts). Between two test cases, print a blank line.

title: Triplet-Sum
description: Given an array and a value, find if there is a triplet in the array whose sum is equal to the given value. If such a triplet exists print "TRUE", else print "FALSE".
inputFormat:
First line contains number of test cases t.
First line of each test case contains N.
Second line contains N space separated integers.
Next line contains X, the target sum.
outputFormat: For each test case print in a new line "TRUE" if a triplet with the given sum exists in the array, otherwise print "FALSE".

title: Max words in a card
description:
Manju’s 21st birthday is coming up in 10 days. Ukesh plans to give Manju a new iPhone that is recently launched. He buys the gift and packs it in a wrapper. He wants to attach a greeting card with the gift. He visits a store which has a great collection of greeting cards. Looking at all the good collections that are available, Ukesh gets confused and decides to pick a greeting card that has maximum words in it.

The store has a total of N cards and each card has a sentence S in it. The sentence consists of alphabets and spaces.

Ukesh is unaware of the maximum words present in a single card among N cards. Help Ukesh by telling him the maximum number of words that a card has among N different cards.

Note: Do not use inbuilt functions.
inputFormat:
First line of input contains N, which denotes the number of cards.
Next N lines each contain a sentence S representing the greeting message on each card.
outputFormat: A single integer representing the maximum number of words present in a card among N different greeting cards.

title: Spiral Traversal of Matrix
description: Given an N x M matrix, print the elements of the matrix in spiral order. Spiral traversal is a method of traversing all elements of a matrix in a spiral pattern, starting from the top-left corner and moving in a clockwise direction until all elements are visited.
inputFormat:
First line of input contains a single integer T - Number of Test Cases.
First line of each test case contains two integers N and M - number of rows and columns.
Next line contains N x M space separated integers representing elements of the matrix.

Input reading is handled by hidden code; you only need to complete the given function.
outputFormat:
Print the elements of the matrix in spiral order.

This is a functional problem; output printing is handled by hidden code. Return a list containing the spiral traversal of the matrix.

title: Missing Pair
description:
Rita is the owner of a shoe-store. She has a variety of shoes and arranges them in pairs. One day, while cleaning, she found that a shoe was missing from a pair. She suspects that the salesperson has stolen it.

Help Rita find the missing shoe.
inputFormat:
The first line is an integer T - the number of test cases.
For each test case:
- The first line contains an integer N - the number of shoes.
- The next N lines each contain an integer representing the model of the shoes.
outputFormat:
For each test case, print the type of shoe that does not have a pair, in a new line.
Each test case has exactly one such shoe.
constraints:
1 <= T <= 10
1 <= N <= 10^4
1 <= model <= 10^4
N will be an odd number.
T * N will always be less than 10^4.
